steps.title: Translate the first sentence
steps.content:
"Carrie has 4 times as many dollars as Marjani" means Carrie's amount equals 4 times Marjani's amount:
$$c=4m$$

steps.title: Translate the second sentence
steps.content:
"Together they have a total of \$40.00" means their combined amount equals 40:
$$c+m=40$$

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $c=4m$ and $c+m=40$: matches both translations. Keep.
  • B) $m=4c$ and $c+m=40$: this would mean Marjani has 4 times Carrie's money — reversed. Eliminate.
  • C) $c=40m$ and $c+m=4$: wrong multiplier and wrong total. Eliminate.
  • D) $m=40c$ and $c+m=4$: reversed and wrong total. Eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
